Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## GKE upgrade process causes performance issues for customer **Question:** Hello, A question of the customer: They have a GKE cluster with 4 nodes, running a large amount of Java Spring applications. While in "running state" the resources are more than enough, when upgrades happen, 20+ applications on one node are rescheduled all at the same time to a new node.
With Spring being resource-hungry at startup, apps throttle, they cant start in time, readiness/liveness start failing and they get restart of pods constantly until 1 by 1 they start, but this takes time. Is there any setting on tip to mitigate this? **Answer:** Hi dariobanfi@
How come 20+ applications are being reassigned to 1 node out of 4? I'm guessing they are using `Surge` Upgrade strategy which (by default) creates only 1 extra node at a time? Please take a look here. Instead of `maxSurge=1` (default), they can use `maxSurge=2 or 3`. If they still see the same issue then, increase this parameter again.. 4 or 5. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#optimizing-surge Alternative solution can be the `blue/green` upgrade strategy. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-pool-upgrade-strategies#blue-green-upgrade-strategy In `Blue/Green` upgrades, GKE creates equal number of extra nodes (~ 4 nodes in this case). cordon and drain the old nodes and reassigns applications to these new 4 nodes. You have much more control and flexibility with `Blue/Green` as compared to `Surge`. Hope it helps! --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
We wanted to share our experience with a GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout and lessons learnt.
Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

Please find below the executive summary and full details in the [post mortem document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fX4FK8mS235DDei_0ttNb9hvNXgZ8YRSrUbBLCT7cn4/edit?usp=sharing) **Executive Summary**
**Impact** - Broadcom BlazeMeter were not able to perform a manual upgrade of prod+staging GKE clusters to apply a security patch since Sep 16, 2020 until Oct 5, 2020 **Root Cause** - GKE clusters upgrade failed in Us-east4-b due to a stockout of RAM for N1-standard-8 Machines **Resolution** - BM workloads were migrated to a different cell with available resources **Answer:** ## Postmortem lessons learned **GKE upgrade might require additional capacity for a limited time** * As GKE does not support in-place upgrade, new nodes are spinned up and once installed, old nodes are removed (see [“surge upgrades”](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/cluster-upgrades#surge) for more details)
* It is recommended for TAM to validate prior to GKE upgrade capacity is available for required machine shapes **Reservations are not a solution for stock outs** if there are ongoing capacity constraints in the specific region, zone, cell **Capacity issues playbook** (capacity team)
* Need to check capacity availability for all GCE / GKE components (Cores, VM family and machine shape, Storage, RAM and other related products)
* Check stockout details by investigating the error messages that can be captured by the support team - Capacity 360 provides very limited data related to a stockout event.
* Improve capacity planning tools and systems to capture RAM signals **Support team** * Communicate all related information to the account team and capacity planning team related to Machine shapes, RAM requirements and storage needs. * Improve Support Stockout playbook to contain comprehensive information on Cloud AC stockout issues and potential root causes (current link: ) **TAM**
* TAM should be thorough: TAM scheduled an additional upgrade without a root cause identified; TAM should make sure to identify root cause prior to scheduling an additional upgrade attempt
* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,

* Make sure to create a chat room for a P1 case for continuity and transparency between TSE/capacity/account team Encourage BCOM BlazeMeter team to **share any major updates for on demand capacity usage** so we can proactively review the availability and mitigate any risks upfront --- ## [Lessons learned] GKE upgrade delayed due to a stockout **Question:** Hey all TAMs,
